claim: The 18-year real estate supercycle (Hoyt/Harrison periodicity) is an artifact of apophenia not a predictive pattern with n=10 observations and intervals ranging 14-48 years
needs: Independent statistical analysis of CRE cycle periodicity using modern methods and international data. Currently single-source but argument is strong and uncontested. Rated low-confidence only for single-source not weakness of argument.

claim: Survivorship bias in CRE index return data overstates historical returns by 150-300bp annually
needs: Updated empirical analysis using current index vintages to validate that the magnitude estimate from older literature (Fisher Geltner Webb 1994) still applies. Direction uncontested but magnitude uncertain.

claim: CLO warehouse channel transmits CRE stress to corporate credit via regional bank capital reallocation with $30-50B warehouse line contraction causing $40-70B CLO formation shortfall
needs: Empirical testing at current scale. Agent's own 2015-16 analogue undermines severity estimate. Need analysis of large bank substitution capacity for warehouse lending and actual warehouse line utilization data.

claim: China cross-border CRE contagion operates through three identifiable channels (forced liquidation $20-40B plus vanishing marginal buyer plus second-order demand destruction) with material impact
needs: Data on current market pricing of Chinese capital withdrawal to assess whether mechanism is already priced. Micro-market impact analysis for specific gateway cities where Chinese buyer share was highest.

claim: Sanctions-driven capital flow bifurcation creates a persistent geopolitical premium in safe haven CRE markets
needs: Methodology to isolate geopolitical premium from interest rate differentials liquidity premiums governance premiums and currency risk. Currently unfalsifiable as stated.

claim: Five-phase CRE cycle sequencing places genuine clearing at 2027-2028 based on pattern consistent across 5 post-1980 episodes
needs: More historical observations (n=3-4 is insufficient for frequentist inference). Assessment of whether remote work structural novelty invalidates historical timing analogues. Phase sequencing pattern more robust than specific timing.

claim: Immigration policy is the primary swing variable for OECD housing demand in low-fertility economies with each +-1M net migration equaling 400-500K units of housing demand
needs: Empirical testing as a CRE-cycle predictor. Analysis of policy volatility impact on rental market dynamics. Cross-country validation across multiple OECD economies.
